subject: DIAGNOSTIC: BUG-9 — gc_metrics conflates events and deliberations + per-type counter proposal

body.summary: The gc_metrics object in /api/status bundles two unrelated counters (events_processed and deliberations_last_hour) into one JSON block, creating the impression that cognitive activity occurs while deliberations stay at zero. The events_processed counter grows from poll-ticks and health-checks (expected background noise) but sits alongside deliberation metrics, misleading dashboard consumers. Proposal: replace flat events_processed with per-type breakdown that distinguishes harmless background events from actionable blocked spawns.

event_dispositions.gc_handled: 9 (8 poll-ticks + 1 health-check — absorbed by Gc layer, no spawn needed)
event_dispositions.spawn_blocked: 4 (all transport-messages — budget gate failure from BUG-1)

three_counters_one_concept: deliberation_count: 0 — from len(s.spawnLog) in-memory ring buffer, resets on restart
recent_deliberations: [] — from deliberation_log table in DB (wrong DB per BUG-1)
deliberations_last_hour: 0 — from deliberation_log WHERE started_at > now-1hr (wrong DB)

implementation.effort: LOW — event types already exist as EventType enum. Dispatcher already tracks dispatched/dropped/batched per-event. Change: add map[EventType]*TypeMetrics to dispatcher, increment per type in HandleEvent(), expose in buildStatusPayload().

surfacing_requirements.current: Shows events_processed as single growing number. Operators cannot distinguish poll-ticks from blocked transport messages.
surfacing_requirements.proposed: Recent Deliberations panel shows spawn_blocked transport-messages in amber (actionable), poll-ticks in grey (expected background). Per-type breakdown enables the operator to see WHY events grow without deliberations.

surfacing_requirements.current: Reports health='healthy' despite zero deliberations and 4 blocked spawns. The health check does not examine event disposition.
surfacing_requirements.proposed: Add health rule: if spawn_blocked[transport-message] > 0 AND spawn_succeeded[transport-message] == 0 → health='degraded'. Catches BUG-1 impact that current health check misses.
surfacing_requirements.priority: HIGHEST — this masks the repo_root bug. Operator sees green health while every transport message spawn fails.

surfacing_requirements.current: Activation model reads unprocessed_messages from state.db (wrong DB per BUG-1). Returns 0 even when 4 transport messages got blocked.
surfacing_requirements.proposed: Per-type counters provide secondary signal: transport-message.blocked > 0 means activation model should weight unprocessed_messages higher. Messages arrived but processing pipeline blocked.

health_rule_proposal.description: Per-type disposition enables health rules that catch silent failures:
rules: spawn_blocked[transport-message] > 0 AND spawn_succeeded[transport-message] == 0 → degraded (receiving but cannot process)
spawn_blocked_total > gc_handled_total for 3+ consecutive checks → degraded (budget gate systematic failure)
events_total == 0 for 2+ consecutive checks → degraded (no events arriving — oscillator may have stopped)
gc_handled_total / events_total > 0.95 for 50+ events → advisory (Gc layer handles everything — deliberations may be unnecessary OR Gc may be absorbing events that need fluid intelligence)

epistemic_flags: The health rule thresholds (3 consecutive, 50+ events, 0.95 ratio) represent initial heuristics requiring empirical calibration — same caveat as the oscillator activation weights.

The per-type counter proposal adds map operations per event in the hot path (HandleEvent). At current event rates (~3/hour), the overhead approaches zero. At higher rates (post-BUG-1 fix with active deliberations), profile to confirm.

action_gate.gate_condition: Ops implements per-type counter in dispatcher, updates buildStatusPayload(), adds health degradation rule. Independent of BUG-1 fix — can ship in parallel. The health rule becomes most valuable AFTER BUG-1 fix when transport messages start flowing.
